message: 🎨 ACMM: self-rendered badge preview (no shields.io dependency) (#8418) * 🎨 acmm: self-rendered badge preview instead of shields.io img The badge preview in the Get Badge panel was loading an from shields.io, which depended on an external service and could show a broken image when shields.io cached a timeout or the first-ever scan of a repo was slow. Replace with a locally-rendered BadgePreview component that mirrors the shields.io two-tone pill layout using the scan data we already have in context. The preview is now instant and never breaks. The copy-paste markdown/HTML snippets still reference shields.io (for the user's README) — only the in-dashboard preview changed. Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson * 🔧 acmm: fix ui-ux-standard hex color ratchet in BadgePreview Add ai-quality-ignore to intentional hex color constants (they mirror shields.io badge colors and can't use CSS vars). Replace bg-[#555] with bg-neutral-600 tailwind class. Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson --------- Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson

files.patch:
@@ -18,6 +18,38 @@ const REPO_RE = /^[\w.-]+\/[\w.-]+$/ const BADGE_SITE = 'https://console.kubestellar.io' const COPIED_FEEDBACK_MS = 1500 +/** shields.io color bands by level — must match acmm-badge.mts LEVEL_COLORS. + * Hex values are intentional: this renders inline badge SVG-style colors. */ +const BADGE_COLORS: Record = { + 1: '#9e9e9e', // lightgrey // ai-quality-ignore + 2: '#dfb317', // yellow // ai-quality-ignore + 3: '#97ca00', // yellowgreen // ai-quality-ignore + 4: '#44cc11', // brightgreen // ai-quality-ignore + 5: '#7b64ff', // blueviolet // ai-quality-ignore +} + +/** Locally-rendered badge preview — mirrors the shields.io two-tone pill + * so the preview is instant and doesn't depend on an external service. */ +function BadgePreview({ level, levelName, detected, total }: { + level: number + levelName: string + detected: number + total: number +}) { + const rightColor = BADGE_COLORS[level] ?? BADGE_COLORS[1] + return ( + + ACMM + + L{level} · {levelName} · {detected}/{total} + + + ) +} + export function RepoPicker() { const { repo, setRepo, recentRepos, scan, openIntro } = useACMM() const [input, setInput] = useState(repo) @@ -187,7 +219,12 @@ export function RepoPicker() { {showBadge && (
